mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income security and rising recession risk; higher unemployment momentum reduces expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was rising into the January 2009 decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered walk-forward periods with positive 3-month UNRATE changes deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the slope coefficient is not negative at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing of FEDFUNDS into an easing regime lowers discount rates and signals central-bank support; after the crossing, valuation support dominates near-term earnings damage and produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below its 6-month moving average after declining at least 50bp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:policy rates had been cut aggressively before 2009-01-15
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following qualifying FEDFUNDS easing crossings are not higher than unconditional 60-day returns, or if the estimated effect is negative in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in RSAFS indicates immediate consumer demand contraction; when retail sales fall below their 12-month moving average, expected revenue growth weakens and the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:retail sales were contracting sharply before the January 2009 decision date
failure_condition: Reject if crossing events show a nonnegative mean 20-day excess return versus non-crossing months or if the hit rate for negative 20-day returns is below 50% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 6-month trend in INDPRO measures broad production cuts; falling industrial output reduces operating leverage and cyclicals' earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 6-month INDPRO change has a nonnegative coefficient for subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward regressions, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 in absolute value over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies accelerating labor-market stress; this joint deterioration compresses household spending and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 3 months and UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:payroll employment was contracting and unemployment was rising before 2009-01-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either PAYEMS decline or UNRATE rise alone, or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead construction employment, durable-goods demand, and credit losses; when HOUST crosses below a prior-year trough, the housing shock transmits to earnings and produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its lowest value from the prior 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts had collapsed before the January 2009 decision date
failure_condition: Reject if prior-12-month downside breakouts in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is falling while FEDFUNDS is declining, disinflation and easing jointly signal demand weakness rather than a benign valuation boost; the interaction predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines over 3 months and FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:inflation pressure and policy rates were falling into early 2009
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-decline and FEDFUNDS-decline interaction has a positive coefficient for 20-day returns, or if triggered returns are not below returns during easing without CPIAUCSL declines.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in real activity after a contraction regime produces short-horizon relief rallies; when GDPC1 and INDPRO both turn upward from recent declines, expected recession depth falls and S&P 500 5-day returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is positive and INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after both were negative
evidence_refs: fact:real activity indicators were central to recession assessment before 2009-01-15
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive turns in GDPC1 and INDPRO after prior declines do not generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the joint signal underperforms INDPRO alone.
